steps.explanation: Small tanks hold 1 fish each, so \(s\) small tanks hold \(s \cdot 1 = s\) fish. Large tanks hold 2 fish each, so \(b\) large tanks hold \(b \cdot 2 = 2b\) fish.
The total number of fish is the sum from both tank types: \(s + 2b = 37\).

Evaluate each choice:
  • Choice A: \(s + 2b = 37\). Each small tank contributes 1 fish and each large tank contributes 2 fish, matching the setup exactly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • Choice B: \(s + b = 37\). This would mean every tank, regardless of size, holds exactly 1 fish. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: \(2s + b = 37\). This reverses the capacities, assigning 2 fish to small tanks and 1 fish to large tanks. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: \(2s + 2b = 37\). This assumes every tank holds 2 fish, ignoring the small-tank capacity of 1. Incorrect.
